candidate.description: Algorithms enabling unreliable distributed nodes to agree on a single value despite failures and partitions; covers Paxos, Raft, Zab, and Byzantine variants.

candidates_full.full_description: Family of algorithms (Paxos, Raft, Zab, PBFT) by which a set of unreliable nodes can agree on a single value or sequence of values despite crashes, network partitions, or in some variants Byzantine behavior. Foundational to replicated state machines.
Selecting a single coordinator among replicas; often a sub-step of consensus protocols.
